title: aira caryophyllea subsp. patulipes
intro: Aira caryophyllea subsp. patulipes, also known as the Wood Meadow Bent, is a subspecies of grass within the genus Aira. It is a type of flowering plant that is native to Europe and Asia. Aira caryophyllea subsp. patulipes has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to provide health benefits such as soothing digestive issues and reducing inflammation.
